children.title: Viable Network
children.label: InTouch can become a commercially viable managed referral network for complementary service providers.

children.title: Initial Wedge
children.label: The initial startup wedge can support a working managed referral-circle model.

children.title: Wedge Fit
children.label: Newly founded or recently funded startups are a viable customer-profile wedge for multi-provider referral circles.

children.title: Service Window
children.label: Newly founded or recently funded startups create overlapping service windows where multiple complementary provider types can serve the same customer profile.

children.title: Referral Situations
children.label: (P0) Providers encounter situations where their customers need another circle member’s service.

children.title: Low Competition
children.label: Provider types are non-competitive enough to collaborate without threatening each other’s business.

children.title: Referral Value
children.label: Expected referral value is high enough for providers to care about improving referral flow.

children.title: Lead Gap
children.label: Providers lack an efficient, reliable source of qualified referral leads from complementary service providers.

children.title: Referrer Shortage
children.label: (P0) Many providers do not already have enough complementary providers who can refer them relevant customers.

children.title: Costly Alternatives
children.label: (P0) Existing referral-networking options require too much work, skill, or risk relative to the lead value they produce.

children.title: Mechanism Value
children.label: (P0) At least one InTouch value mechanism creates enough value in this wedge.

children.title: Circle Formation
children.label: (P0) Curated circle formation creates enough value by helping providers join a better referral group than they could form themselves.

children.title: Referral Generation
children.label: (P0) Managed referral generation creates enough value in this wedge.

children.title: Credible Source
children.label: (P0) Complementary providers are a credible source of qualified leads because they encounter relevant customer needs before or during their own sales or service work.

children.title: Incremental Leads
children.label: (P0) Managed referral circles generate qualified leads that would not have happened otherwise.

children.title: Opportunity Workflow
children.label: Referral opportunity management creates enough value by helping providers notice and follow through on valuable intro moments.

children.title: Opportunity Surfacing
children.label: Referral opportunity surfacing creates enough value by helping providers notice valuable intro moments they would otherwise miss.

children.title: Followthrough Lift
children.label: Referral coordination and accountability creates enough value by increasing follow-through after a referral opportunity is identified.

children.title: Can Execute
children.label: At least one valuable InTouch mechanism can be delivered well enough in this wedge.

children.title: Circle Composition
children.label: InTouch can identify promising circle compositions with enough accuracy.

children.title: Opportunity Quality
children.label: InTouch can identify and surface qualified referral opportunities with enough accuracy, timing, and context.

children.title: Support Coordination
children.label: InTouch can support referral coordination without requiring too much manual effort from members.

children.title: Behavior Adoption
children.label: Providers and circles repeatedly perform the behaviors required for the network to work.

children.title: Complete Setup
children.label: Providers complete the initial circle setup steps needed to make matching and referrals useful.

children.title: Share Context
children.label: Providers share enough customer and relationship context for referral opportunities to be identified.

children.title: Referral Trust
children.label: Providers trust circle members and surfaced referral opportunities enough to act on them.

children.title: Act Quickly
children.label: (P0) Providers act on credible referral opportunities when surfaced.

children.title: Report Outcomes
children.label: Providers report enough referral outcomes to maintain accountability and learning.

children.title: Stay Active
children.label: (P0) Referral circles remain active across repeated customer interactions, not just onboarding.

children.title: Repeatable Growth
children.label: There is a repeatable way to form and grow viable referral circles in this wedge.

children.title: Reach Channels
children.label: Repeatable channels can reach providers who sell to newly founded or recently funded startups.

children.title: Compelling Proposition
children.label: The value proposition generates qualified interest from multiple complementary provider types.

children.title: Interest Conversion
children.label: (P0) Interested providers convert into actual circle participation.

children.title: Independent Formation
children.label: Circle formation can be repeated without relying on founder-only personal networks.

children.title: Member Expansion
children.label: Existing members create a credible path to additional qualified members or adjacent circles.

children.title: Economic Viability
children.label: This wedge can capture enough value after acquisition and operating costs.

children.title: Will Pay
children.label: Providers will pay enough for circle formation, referral value, or workflow improvement.

children.title: Acquisition Economics
children.label: The cost of identifying, recruiting, and assembling circles can support attractive acquisition economics.

children.title: Operating Economics
children.label: The managed operating model can deliver referral value without requiring too much manual work per circle.

children.title: Trusted Pricing
children.label: Pricing does not damage trust, referral behavior, or perceived neutrality in circles.

children.title: Business Path
children.label: The initial startup wedge creates a credible path to a meaningful business beyond first proof.

children.title: Standalone Potential
children.label: The startup wedge is large and economically attractive enough to support a meaningful business on its own.

children.title: Segment Scalability
children.label: The startup wedge can expand across similar startup segments, provider clusters, or geographies.

children.title: Adjacent Expansion
children.label: The startup wedge creates a credible path into adjacent customer-profile wedges.

children.title: Model Portability
children.label: The managed referral-circle model proven in the startup wedge can be replicated in other customer-profile wedges.

children.title: Clustered Needs
children.label: Other customer profiles also create clustered needs for multiple complementary service providers.

children.title: Supply Gap
children.label: Providers serving other customer profiles also lack efficient, reliable sources of qualified referral leads from complementary providers.

children.title: Mechanism Portability
children.label: The selected InTouch value mechanism can create referral-network value outside the startup wedge.

children.title: Formation Portability
children.label: The method for identifying and assembling promising circles can work beyond startup-focused providers.

children.title: Trust Friction
children.label: InTouch can operate with acceptable privacy, trust, and data-sharing friction across wedges.

children.title: Privacy Manageable
children.label: Providers can share enough customer and relationship context for InTouch to work without unacceptable privacy concerns.

children.title: Customer Safety
children.label: Customers are not exposed to referral behavior that damages provider trust or customer relationships.

children.title: Member Trust
children.label: Circle members trust how InTouch uses shared data and maintains neutrality between members.
